name: smith one arm row
instructions: Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to waist level.
Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Grasp the bar with one hand using an overhand grip, with your palm facing down.
Keep your elbow close to your body and pull the bar towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to waist level. Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight. Grasp the bar with one hand using an overhand grip, with your palm facing down. Keep your elbow close to your body and pull the bar towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
